---
name: Task Assignment
about: Assign a task to RA or community member (maintainers only)
title: '[TASK] '
labels: task
assignees: ''
---

## 1. Objective
<!-- What needs to be accomplished -->


## 2. Background & Importance
<!-- Why this task matters and where it fits in the broader project -->


## 3. Input
<!-- Source files, reference materials, data locations -->
- **Source files**: 
- **Reference materials**: 
- **Related issues/PRs**: 


## 4. Expected Output
<!-- Format, structure, and quality standards -->
### Deliverables
- [ ] 

### Quality Standards
- [ ] 


## 5. Timeline
<!-- Key dates and milestones -->
- **Start date**: 
- **Check-in point** (if applicable): 
- **Deadline**: 


## 6. Decision Rules
<!-- How to handle uncertainties: what can be decided independently vs. what requires asking -->
### You can decide on your own:
- 

### Must ask before proceeding:
- 


## 7. Definition of Done
<!-- Clear criteria for task completion -->
- [ ] All deliverables completed
- [ ] Code/output passes sanity checks
- [ ] Documentation is complete
- [ ] Changes are reproducible (see checklist below)


## 8. Progress Report Template
<!-- Use this format when reporting progress -->
When updating this issue, please include:

```markdown
### What was done
- 

### Output location
- 

### Problems encountered
- 

### Next steps recommended
- 
```

---

## Reproducibility Checklist (for code/data tasks)
<!-- Ensure all work is reproducible -->
- [ ] Raw data is not modified (create `clean/` or `processed/` instead)
- [ ] Each step has its own script/notebook
- [ ] Master script can run end-to-end
- [ ] Folder structure follows convention: `raw/`, `clean/`, `temp/`, `output/`, `code/`
- [ ] All manual corrections are documented with justification
- [ ] Changelog updated for each significant change

---

## Notes for Task Creator
<!-- Self-checklist for task creators (remove this section when creating the issue) -->
Before creating this task, verify:
- [ ] Task is broken into manageable pieces
- [ ] Success criteria are explicit
- [ ] Deadline is realistic with buffer time
- [ ] RA knows what they can decide vs. what to escalate

<!--
If this is the first task for a new RA, consider:
1. Start with a smaller test task
2. Clearly label it as a "test task" to establish quality baseline
3. Schedule a mid-point check-in (week 1 check-in)
-->
